prompt:
The seedling-house vent controller stopped linking and I cannot see why. Every translation unit compiles clean -- not one warning under -Wall -Wextra -Werror -- and then the link step falls over:

    $ make test
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c test_vent.c
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -c vent.c
    gcc -std=c17 -Wall -Wextra -Werror -o test_vent test_vent.o vent.o
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: test_vent.o: in function `test_steps_are_rate_limited_both_ways':
    test_vent.c:(.text+0x546): undefined reference to `vent_step_toward'
    /usr/bin/ld.bfd: test_vent.o: in function `test_plan_walks_to_the_target':
    test_vent.c:(.text+0x91c): undefined reference to `vent_plan'
    collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status

vent_step_toward and vent_plan are right there in vent.c. grep finds both definitions, my editor jumps to both, the header declares both, and vent.c itself compiles without a peep. vent_target_percent from the very same file links fine, which is the part that breaks my brain.

The vent maths is tuned and must not change -- proportional band, rate-limited ticks, capped plans, all pinned by the tests. Get make test passing without touching test_vent.c, mintest.h, or the Makefile.
